claim: A clear system prompt defining the agent's role, capabilities, and constraints is essential for consistent behavior in agentic codebases
actionable: Write a comprehensive system prompt that defines your agent's role, available tools, decision-making guidelines, and behavioral constraints
source_title: One Prompt Every AGENTIC Codebase Should Have (For Engineering Teams)

claim: The system prompt should explicitly enumerate what the agent can and cannot do to prevent unintended behaviors
actionable: Include explicit guardrails and constraints in the system prompt: 'You cannot...', 'You will always...', 'You must validate...'
source_title: One Prompt Every AGENTIC Codebase Should Have (For Engineering Teams)

claim: System prompts for agents should specify how to handle errors, edge cases, and when to escalate decisions to humans
actionable: Include error handling guidance and escalation criteria in the system prompt: 'If you encounter X error, do Y', 'Escalate to human when...'
source_title: One Prompt Every AGENTIC Codebase Should Have (For Engineering Teams)

claim: Well-designed system prompts reduce hallucinations and off-task behavior by providing clear guardrails for agent reasoning
actionable: Test your system prompts with adversarial examples to ensure they effectively constrain agent behavior and reduce hallucinations
source_title: One Prompt Every AGENTIC Codebase Should Have (For Engineering Teams)
